symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden severe tearing left-flank pain escalating from chronic ache
relieving_factors: lying still on the right side

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sudden capsular-stretch pain from acute cyst haemorrhage/rupture.
Constant, non-colicky, non-radiating pain is less typical of a passing stone.
Bleeding cyst communicating with the collecting system produces visible blood in urine.
Heavy haematuria can clot and obstruct urine flow.

symptoms.symptom_name: Low-grade fever / feeling hot and lethargic

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fever with flank pain in ADPKD raises concern for cyst infection with urosepsis potential.

symptoms.symptom_name: Massive firm bilateral flank masses (enlarged polycystic kidneys)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpable bilateral flank masses reflect grossly enlarged cystic kidneys — a clinician palpation finding, not obtainable via telehealth.
Localised renal-angle tenderness localises the acute pathology to the left kidney — requires clinician examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Point-of-care urinalysis: gross blood, trace leukocytes, no nitrites

investigation_details.investigation_name: Urinalysis (point-of-care) and formal urine microscopy and culture
investigation_details.expected_result: Frank blood, trace leukocytes without nitrites; formal microscopy and culture needed to confirm or exclude infection
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Cyst infection may go unrecognised and untreated.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms macroscopic haematuria; clinician/lab test, not obtainable via telehealth in real time.

symptoms.symptom_name: CT KUB: massively enlarged kidneys replaced by innumerable anechoic/low-density cysts

investigation_details.investigation_name: Non-contrast CT KUB (renal ultrasound as alternative)
investigation_details.expected_result: Bilaterally massively enlarged kidneys entirely replaced by innumerable cysts; may show intra-cystic haemorrhage or complicated cyst
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Cannot characterise haemorrhage vs infection or exclude obstruction/significant bleed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Imaging is required to characterise the complication and exclude significant haemorrhage or obstruction — never available in real time via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known ADPKD with strong family history of end-stage kidney failure

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Established diagnosis with autosomal-dominant inheritance pattern anchors the underlying condition.
Early-onset hypertension is a hallmark complication of ADPKD; the measured value is a clinician/device measurement not obtained via telehealth here.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Acute cyst complication in known ADPKD
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Sudden severe flank pain + gross haematuria + fever on a known ADPKD background is a high-risk acute complication requiring urgent in-person assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count (haemoglobin) and coagulation profile
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess for significant haemorrhage / haemoglobin drop from a bleeding cyst.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: U&E, creatinine, eGFR and inflammatory markers (CRP)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess renal function and infection/inflammation in a high-risk polycystic kidney.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Formal urine microscopy, culture and sensitivities
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirm or exclude cyst infection guiding antibiotic need.
Characterise cyst haemorrhage vs infection and exclude obstruction or significant retroperitoneal bleed.
NSAIDs are contraindicated — nephrotoxic and worsen cyst bleeding; the AI must not recommend them.
